next_steps: Perform nerve biopsy (e.g., sural nerve) to evaluate for vasculitis
Repeat detailed neurological examination after pain control is optimized
Consult rheumatology for evaluation of active systemic inflammatory joint disease
Consider skin biopsy to assess for leukocytoclastic vasculitis
Neuropsychological evaluation to assess cognitive impairment
Re-evaluate for NPH via formal gait analysis if physical therapy and pain management allow

differential_diagnoses: Systemic onset rheumatoid arthritis with secondary neuropathy
next_steps: Obtain comprehensive rheumatologic serologies (RF, anti-CCP, ANA, ANCA, ESR, CRP, Complement levels)
Consult Rheumatology for evaluation of active inflammatory polyarthritis and systemic vasculitis assessment
Perform diagnostic arthrocentesis of an affected joint for synovial fluid analysis
Perform nerve conduction study/electromyography follow-up and proceed with sural nerve biopsy if vasculitis is suspected
Order CT chest, abdomen, and pelvis to evaluate for occult malignancy
Complete vitamin B6 replacement and monitor clinical response
Continue aggressive pain management and perform follow-up neurological examination
